page: Google Cloud Platform c4-highmem-4-lssd is an x86_64 architecture server powered by an Intel Xeon 6985P-C processor. It features 4 dedicated vCPUs derived from 2 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, operating at a base clock speed of 2.3 GHz. The system is equipped with 31.0 GB of system memory, yielding 7.75 GB of RAM per vCPU. Storage is provided via a bundled 137 GB local NVMe SSD, while no dedicated GPU hardware is included.

Benchmark data reveals a contrast between single-core and multi-core performance. The instance achieves top-tier results in single-threaded gzip compression at 35.55 GB/sec and Geekbench text processing. However, multi-core metrics, such as the Geekbench multi-core score of 4215.0 and stress-ng multi-core CPU performance, fall into the bottom 25%. Memory bandwidth is also constrained, with read-only large block bandwidth at 22.46 GB/sec and write large block bandwidth at 15.13 GB/sec, both ranking in the lower quartile.

This server configuration is suited for memory-intensive, single-threaded tasks or workloads that benefit from local NVMe storage, such as caching layers, lightweight database operations, and text processing. The high memory-to-vCPU ratio provides qualitative cost efficiency for applications requiring a substantial memory footprint without needing high parallel compute power. However, the weak multi-core scaling and lower memory bandwidth represent significant tradeoffs for highly parallelized workloads, heavy compilation, or large-scale database operations.
description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highmem-4-lssd is an x86_64 server configured with 4 dedicated vCPUs from an Intel Xeon 6985P-C processor running at 2.3 GHz, 31.0 GB of RAM, and a bundled 137 GB local NVMe SSD. It lacks a dedicated GPU. The instance exhibits strong single-core performance, achieving top-tier results in single-threaded gzip compression and text processing. Conversely, multi-core performance and memory bandwidth are limited, falling into the bottom 25% across several benchmarks, including Geekbench multi-core and stress-ng multi-core tests. This server offers a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 7.75 GB per core, making it qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-bound workloads that do not require intensive parallel processing. It is suited for lightweight database operations, caching, and single-threaded text processing tasks.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highmem-4-lssd features 4 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 31 GB RAM, and a 137 GB local NVMe SSD. It offers strong single-core performance but limited multi-core scaling.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highmem-4-lssd is an x86_64 server with 4 vCPUs, 31 GB RAM, and a 137 GB local NVMe SSD, optimized for memory-heavy tasks.
tagline: A memory-dense virtual server featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors and local NVMe SSD storage for single-threaded and memory-bound workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 6985P-C processor with 4 dedicated vCPUs and 31 GB RAM
Bundled 137 GB local NVMe SSD for low-latency storage needs
Top-tier single-threaded performance in text processing and gzip compression
Lower multi-core performance and memory bandwidth relative to hardware class
Suited for caching, lightweight databases, and memory-intensive single-threaded tasks
